_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Presence of symptomatic renal cysts (manifesting as fever, pain/tenderness between ribs and pelvis, upper abdominal pain, changes in urinary habits, or hematuria).
Symptoms are not relieved by analgesic medication or needle aspiration in the long term.
Absence of suspicion of malignancy based on imaging (e.g., thin walls, no calcification, septation, or enhancement on contrast studies).

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication for procedure, and planned line of management

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical findings and USG / CT scan report indicative of the procedure?

common_queries: Clinical findings and/or USG/CT scan report are not indicative of the procedure.
Clinical notes (including evaluation findings, indication, and planned line of management) not submitted or incomplete at pre-authorization.
USG/CT report not submitted or incomplete at pre-authorization.
Diagnosis not supported by documented clinical manifestations (fever, pain, upper abdominal pain, urinary changes, hematuria).
Lack of documentation regarding prior conservative management or aspiration failure.
Suspicion of malignancy not ruled out by clinical findings or imaging.

clinical_key_pointers: Proceed with Renal Cyst deroofing or Marsupialization only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical manifestation:
